semantic_description: Scans input string s from left to right until the terminating 0, copies every character not equal to c into caller-provided output buffer out, writes a terminating 0 after the copied prefix, and returns the number of copied characters. The input string memory is preserved. The output memory is modified so its prefix equals string_remove_char_to_output_spec(l, c), followed by 0 and an unconstrained suffix. The main control structure is a while(1) loop with a break on s[i] == 0 and a conditional output write/increment when s[i] != c. Empty input is possible when n == 0.
